macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Raw response
items.summary:
I appreciate your request, but I need to be transparent: I don't have access to real-time web search capabilities or current market data. My knowledge was last updated in April 2024, which is nearly two years before the date you've specified (March 4, 2026).

To get accurate, current macroeconomic and stock market news from the past 24 hours, I recommend:

1. **Financial News Sites**: Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC, MarketWatch, Financial Times
2. **Market Data Platforms**: Yahoo Finance, Google Finan

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Markets Tumble Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly rallied above $70,000 but tumbled, with Ether and Solana down as investors flee riskier assets; XRP dropped 2.6% while Circle's stock surged nearly 7-24%.[1]

items.title: Trump Pressures Banks on Crypto Legislation
items.summary: President Trump urged banks to stop hindering cryptocurrency bills like the Crypto Agenda and Clarity Act, pushing for quick advancement to keep digital asset innovation in the US.[2][3]

items.title: American Bitcoin Company Expands Mining Capacity
items.summary: The Trump-affiliated American Bitcoin Company is expanding mining capacity by 12%, doubling down on a pure mining and hoarding strategy amid market downturns.[1]

items.title: Circle and USDC See Strong Volumes and Growth
items.summary: Circle, issuer of USDC, experienced significant stock gains and rising volumes; its stablecoin entered the top 50,000 with expansion in payments alongside XRP.[1][2]

items.title: Analysts Predict End of Crypto Winter in March
items.summary: Fundstrat's Tom Lee suggests crypto winter is ending, signaling positive momentum for crypto stocks alongside MAG7 and software amid resilient markets despite Iran tensions.[4]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Makes Industrial Robotics AI Official Priority with Intrinsic Acquisition
items.summary: Google consolidates robotics capabilities by absorbing Intrinsic, combining DeepMind AI models, Intrinsic's deployment software, and Google Cloud infrastructure. The move positions Google to offer manufacturers integrated AI-powered robotics solutions, with the general-purpose robotics market projected to reach $370 billion by 2040.

items.title: OpenAI Closes $110 Billion Funding Round at $730 Billion Valuation
items.summary: OpenAI secures major investments from SoftBank ($30 billion), Amazon ($50 billion), and Nvidia ($30 billion). Amazon's investment is contingent on OpenAI achieving AGI or going public, and includes commitments to purchase Amazon's Trainium chips.

items.title: University of Toronto and AMD Launch Major AI & Computing R&D Lab
items.summary: The AMD – U of T Research Lab will focus on developing energy-efficient AI systems, decentralized training methods for massive models, and enterprise-scale data intelligence. AMD plans to launch 100 R&D projects over three years and donated state-of-the-art AI servers to support the initiative.

items.title: Meta Testing AI Shopping Assistant to Compete with ChatGPT and Gemini
items.summary: Meta is rolling out an AI shopping tool to US web users that provides product recommendations with images, prices, and brand details. The feature tailors suggestions based on inferred user data including location and gender.

items.title: Research Shows AI Can Unmask Anonymous Internet Users at Scale
items.summary: Researchers from ETH Zurich, Anthropic, and MATS found that AI agents with internet access can re-identify pseudonymous individuals in minutes with 90% precision. The LLM-based method substantially outperforms previous machine learning approaches.

items.title: UK Government Launches Fundamental AI Research Lab with £40 Million Funding
items.summary: The UK government announced a new Fundamental AI Research Lab backed by £40 million in funding over six years to drive next-generation AI breakthroughs.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Invincible VS Reveals Dupli-Kate as New Playable Character
items.summary: Skybound Entertainment's tag fighting game Invincible VS is featured on Game Informer's cover with the announcement of Dupli-Kate as the latest playable character. The game launches April 30, 2026 on Xbox Series X|S, PlayStation 5, and PC.

items.title: GDC 2026 to Focus on GTA 6 Legacy, AI Integration, and Hardware Optimization
items.summary: The Game Developers Conference 2026 will feature discussions on GTA 6's industry impact, the divisive role of generative AI in game development, and hardware optimization challenges affecting console launches including the PS6.

items.title: Valve Announces Steam Machine Launch for 2026
items.summary: Valve plans to launch the Steam Machine in 2026 with fewer constraints for platform verification compared to Steam Deck, offering another entry point to PC gaming.

items.title: Trump Administration Debates Tencent's Stakes in Riot Games and Epic Games
items.summary: The US government is reportedly considering forced divestment of Tencent's ownership stakes in major American gaming companies including Riot Games and Epic Games.

items.title: Overwatch Receives Nier: Automata Crossover Skins
items.summary: Overwatch is adding a collection of Nier: Automata-themed cosmetic skins to the game.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Unveils MacBook Neo at $599, New MacBook Air with M5 Chip, and iPad Air M4
items.summary: Apple announced a new budget MacBook Neo powered by the A18 Pro chip starting at $599, alongside a refreshed MacBook Air featuring the M5 chip with up to 4x faster AI performance, and a new iPad Air with M4 processor. The company also revealed new MacBook Pro models with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, plus updated Apple Studio Display models.

items.title: Tech Giants Sign Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge for AI Data Centers
items.summary: Google, Microsoft, Meta, Oracle, xAI, OpenAI, and Amazon signed an agreement at the White House to ensure AI data center infrastructure costs are not passed to consumers, addressing concerns about rising electricity prices from resource-intensive operations.

items.title: NVIDIA Temporarily Closes Dubai Offices, Shifts to Remote Work
items.summary: NVIDIA temporarily closed its Dubai offices with employees transitioning to remote work, according to an internal email reviewed by CNBC and cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike.
